rulings.Case Title: MARTHA MIRANDA VS. JESSICA KRIEGER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating Sanctions Against Pltf Martha Miranda
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, line 11. DEFENDANT GESSIKA KRIEGER's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff Martha Miranda GRANTED, Plaintiff failed to comply with the Court's November 6, 2014 order. Request for relief under CCP 473(b) is not properly before the Court. Monetary sanctions granted in the amount of $1,625. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ESAU A ZAMBRANO VS. DAVID DE LEON VALDEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, line DEFENDANT DAVID DE LEON VALDEZ MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication DENIED. This action is based on nuisance in addition to breach of lease. Defendant does not deny and does not provide any evidence that he is not a "tenant" and that his tenancy is not subject to termination based on nuisance. There is a triable issue of fact re: accommodation; reasonableness of accommodation; necessity of accommodation in order to afford defendant and equal opportunity to use and enjoy the dwelling; and plaintiff's refusal to make accommodation. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MILLIE LEE ET AL VS. DANIEL WHITTAKER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, line 6. DEFENDANTS DANIEL WHITTAKER, AND MORGEN VAN BURENS' Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Notice of Termination of Tenancy is fatally defective as it does not comply with CC 1946.1(h). Additionally, Plaintiff failed to initiate the termination of all existing tenancies on the property by service of a written notice of termination of tenancy prior to certification of their Notice of Intent to Withdraw. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 2451 CALIFORNIA STREET, LLC VS. MARIA CARUANA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, February 2, 2015, line 7. DEFENDANTS MARIA CARUANA, THERESA CHANS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Notice and all supporting documents sufficiently apprise the tenants of their re-occupancy rights. Ellis Act does not require that landlord provide notice to tenants in one unit regarding extension of time for withdrawal of other units. Plaintiff notified the Rent Board of the extensions as to each unit. (Defendant's RJN Exs. B, C, D). Defendant fails to cite any authority for the proposition that by continuing to provide housing services (such a cable, reimbursement for cable, light, heat, water, etc. See SFRO 37.2(g)) beyond the expiration of Notice of Termination landlord extended the terms and conditions of defendants' tenancy and failed to withdraw from rental market. = (501/REQ)
